class.name: IcyHttpStream
class.brief_description: Handles HTTP requests and continuous audio streaming from Icecast servers.
class.description: [IcyHttpStream] establishes HTTP connections to Icecast-compatible servers and continuously downloads streaming audio data on a dedicated thread. If an AudioDecoder is assigned, downloaded data chunks are forwarded to it for decoding. Otherwise, the stream remains active and incoming data is discarded. The class can optionally parse ICY metadata embedded in the stream and emits signals for connection state changes and metadata updates.

signal.name: connection_opened
signal.description: Emitted when a connection to the streaming server has been successfully established and data reception begins.

signal.name: connection_closed
signal.description: Emitted when the connection is closed, either intentionally or due to an error. [param result] contains a [enum Result] value describing the reason the connection was closed.

signal.name: metadata_received
signal.description: Emitted when ICY metadata is received from the server. The format and contents of [param metadata] depend on the metadata provided by the stream.

method.name: request
method.description: Sends an HTTP GET request to [param url] and attempts to establish a streaming connection. If the connection succeeds, the stream begins downloading data continuously on a dedicated thread until the connection is closed or interrupted. [param url] specifies the URL of the Icecast stream. [param timeout] specifies the connection timeout in seconds. If [param parse_metadata] is [code]true[/code], ICY metadata embedded in the stream is parsed and exposed through the appropriate signals. [param custom_headers] specifies additional HTTP headers to include in the request such as user agent or bearer token. Each entry must be formatted as [code]"Header-Name: Value"[/code].

method.name: cancel_request
method.description: Cancels the active request or stream, if one exists.

method.name: is_requesting
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if a request is currently active or data is being streamed.

method.name: get_content_mime_type
method.description: Returns the MIME type of the streamed media. (e.g [code]"audio/mpeg"[/code] for MP3 streams)

method.name: get_response_headers
method.description: Returns the raw HTTP response headers.

method.name: get_response_headers_as_dictionary
method.description: Returns the HTTP response headers as a key-value [Dictionary].
